findings.description: u_0004 (parent company Priovant Therapeutics sign-off, locked-footer-only placement) has no corresponding candidate rule at all — an entire compliance/governance fact about where corporate sign-off may appear is missing from the group.

payload.description: Do not add parent company (Priovant Therapeutics, Inc.) sign-off within body sections; it must only appear in the locked footer.

findings.description: The unit explicitly states this rule 'supersedes the Feb 2026 general guide's title-case rule' — a versioning/precedence relation to a prior governing document that is not captured anywhere in the rule's fields (no supersedes/relation facet, no scope facet limiting to patient marketing materials).

The single rule conflates a positive 'must include ® on first mention when generic name present, external-facing only' directive with a distinct negative 'do not repeat ® on subsequent mentions' directive. These have opposite polarity (must vs must_not) and should be split into two related rules rather than merged under one polarity=must.

new_rules.description: Do not repeat ® symbol on subsequent mentions after first/most prominent use.

findings.description: Scope restriction 'external-facing documents only' and 'first/most prominent mention only' are compliance-critical conditional facets not represented as explicit scope/condition fields on the rule, risking silent over-application to internal or repeated mentions.

Generic name format rule (lowercase, parenthetical) is standalone but functionally coupled to the trademark pairing rule (u_0002 references the same generic name usage). No explicit relation link between the two rules is recorded, though they co-depend for correct LISRAYA® (brepocitinib) rendering.
